name: Grommash
shortDescription: Frontline warlord that breaks lanes through brute pressure and impact swings.
shortLore: A conqueror pulled from a dead empire that still answers only to the drumbeat of war.
fullLore: Grommash once carried a shattered empire on his back until the last citadel burned around him. Dragged into the Multiverse by a collapsing battle rite, he now treats every arena as a siege that must end in obedience or ruin.

name: Void Weaver
shortDescription: Control-oriented arcanist built around spatial distortion and battlefield manipulation.
shortLore: She threads reality like cloth and leaves seams in places no one should step.
fullLore: Void Weaver was trained inside a monastery that mapped the fractures between worlds. When the monastery vanished, she learned that absence itself could be shaped into a weapon and a refuge in equal measure.

name: Swiftblade
shortDescription: Tempo duelist with fast entries, quick exits, and punishing precision.
shortLore: He was exiled for ending wars before kings could profit from them.
fullLore: Swiftblade belonged to an order that believed speed was mercy. Banished after cutting down a noble line during a forbidden truce, he now hunts across realms with the same philosophy: the cleanest strike is the one that leaves no second chance.

name: Ironclad
shortDescription: Heavy anchor unit that absorbs pressure and turns attrition into advantage.
shortLore: Every plate on his body was forged from a battlefield he survived.
fullLore: Ironclad was rebuilt so many times that no one remembers the soldier beneath the armor. He marches with a furnace heart and a vow to outlast any force that calls itself inevitable.

name: Frost Mage
shortDescription: Reliable ranged caster that slows the field and stabilizes deck curves.
shortLore: She learned patience from winters that buried entire nations.
fullLore: The Frost Mage guarded the final archive of a world consumed by white silence. She carries that silence with her now, shaping it into spells that preserve what matters and freeze what threatens it.

name: Shadow Assassin
shortDescription: Low-cost execution specialist that thrives on openings and isolated targets.
shortLore: Rumor says the first contract was her own name.
fullLore: Shadow Assassin erased herself from every registry that ever named her. The result is a living rumor that slips between factions, timelines, and loyalties without leaving anything behind except unfinished prayers.

name: Blood Priest
shortDescription: Sacrificial support caster that trades stability for explosive swing turns.
shortLore: He heals with the same ritual he uses to condemn.
fullLore: The Blood Priest comes from a faith that sees pain as the truest ledger of power. He serves no temple now, only the balance sheet of survival, where every miracle must be paid for in full.

name: Earthshaker
shortDescription: High-cost disruptor that shifts the whole board with seismic pressure.
shortLore: He was born when a mountain chose violence instead of stillness.
fullLore: Earthshaker is less a warrior than a fault line given purpose. Entire settlements once moved at the sound of his footsteps, and the arena answers him now with the same trembling obedience.

name: Windrunner
shortDescription: Reactive skirmisher that punishes slow setups and keeps decks fluid.
shortLore: She follows storms because they are the only things fast enough to keep up.
fullLore: Windrunner grew up on the edges of sky bridges where one wrong step meant vanishing into cloud. She still fights that way, turning impossible footing into a home field advantage.

name: Arcane Golem
shortDescription: Stable bruiser forged for efficient board presence and deck consistency.
shortLore: Its maker vanished, but the orders in its core are still warm.
fullLore: Arcane Golem was built to defend a city that no longer exists. Deprived of masters but not purpose, it now advances with the patient certainty of a spell that refuses to expire.

name: Necromancer
shortDescription: Late-pressure spellcaster built for recursion, inevitability, and attrition value.
shortLore: He never raises the dead. He only reminds them what they owe.
fullLore: Necromancer studies endings the way scholars study stars. He believes death is not a boundary but an unpaid contract, and every arena gives him new names to collect from the dark.

name: Ranger
shortDescription: Accessible ranged striker that rewards clean positioning and steady tempo.
shortLore: The wild raised her before any faction could claim her banner.
fullLore: Ranger survived by learning every pattern the forest would reveal and every lie invaders would tell. She treats each world as new terrain to read, master, and defend on her own terms.

name: Paladin
shortDescription: Defensive vanguard that mixes presence, discipline, and reliable mid-cost impact.
shortLore: His oath outlived the kingdom that asked for it.
fullLore: Paladin was sworn to protect a throne that collapsed into civil ash. The crown is gone, but the oath remains, and he now carries it into the Multiverse like a banner no ruin can bury.

name: Illusionist
shortDescription: Deceptive control specialist that creates false threats and punishes bad reads.
shortLore: She never lies directly. She simply edits what others think they saw.
fullLore: Illusionist once entertained emperors by making certainty look foolish. When kingdoms started paying more for confusion than spectacle, she adapted instantly and never looked back.

name: Berserker
shortDescription: Straightforward pressure card that keeps aggressive decks online at low complexity.
shortLore: He calls it fury. Everyone else calls it weather.
fullLore: Berserker comes from the north edge of a world where storms learned to wear skin. He enters battle with nothing hidden, nothing restrained, and nothing that resembles fear.

name: Sniper
shortDescription: Precision agility card that converts setup and spacing into clean removals.
shortLore: He measures battlefields by the distance between heartbeat and silence.
fullLore: Sniper trained in a wasteland where ammunition mattered more than speech. Each shot is the end of a calculation he started minutes earlier, and the arena rarely notices until the answer lands.
